mnemonics.title: 8-Hour Framework Sequence
mnemonics.content: VIC LIDS BCR: Vector Spaces → Introduction → Combinations → Linear Dependence → Independence → Dependence → Subspaces → Basis → Coordinates → Row/Column Space

mnemonics.title: Chalk and Talk Steps
mnemonics.content: T-P-I: Theorem (state clearly) → Proof (step-by-step on board) → Illustration (examples and applications)

mnemonics.title: Subspace Test Reminder
mnemonics.content: Zero Vector (must contain) → Closed under Addition → Closed under Scalar Multiplication

mnemonics.title: Teaching Progression
mnemonics.content: Concrete → Abstract: Start with R^2/R^3 examples, move to polynomials and matrices, then general vector spaces

acronyms.usage: The three-step approach for presenting any major result in chalk and talk teaching
Building lessons where each concept builds on previous knowledge in vector spaces
For teaching: Define → Assume linear combination = 0 → Show all coefficients must be zero → Conclude independence

visualTips.visualization: Draw vectors in R^2: if two vectors point in different directions (not collinear), they're independent. In R^3: three vectors not lying in the same plane are independent.
Visualize span{v₁,v₂} in R² as the entire plane (if independent) or a line (if dependent). Draw the vectors and shade the resulting space.
Draw R² with a line through origin (subspace) vs. a line not through origin (not subspace). Emphasize must contain origin.
Show a matrix and draw arrows indicating how row operations preserve row space (same set of vectors) but can change column relationships.

analogies.analogy: Like LEGO blocks that can build any structure - a basis is the minimum set of 'blocks' that can combine to create any vector in the space.
In R³ you can move in 3 independent directions (x, y, z). Dimension tells you how many independent directions exist in that vector space.
Think of a linear transformation as a machine: you input vectors, it transforms them predictably (preserves addition and scalar multiplication).

analogies.concept: Chalk and Talk as Cooking Show
analogies.analogy: Like watching a chef cook step-by-step - you see every ingredient (concept) added in order, can ask questions, and understand the complete process.
